Change your mind and come closer.

EventText.NameOfScene: Change your mind and come closer.

theScene: With the two of you walking off, your aforementioned walk resumes, though with considerably less levity, the usually chipper huntress carrying on without really looking at you or paying much attention to her surroundings, just forlornly making her way back to her usual spot where she halts with a dispirited sigh, letting you choose what you want to do next while she silently broods.
Amy looks perplexed that you'd want to visit the toxic matango again considering the lukewarm results of your previous meeting, your request getting met with a confused, silent stare from her.

However, she also seems hopeful that things will go differently this time, and so readily takes you through the Caverns once more, unerringly traversing tunnel after corridor and unhesitatingly leading you back to the alcove she previously brought you to.

When you walk within view of its monster girl habitant, the mushroom woman quickly recognizes you and doesn't hide her perplexity at seeing you again, but nevertheless seems to discard her hesitation, greeting you with an odd-looking smile.
He he he... It's you again? To what do I owe the honor? Might you actually be willing to entertain this lone lady's want for company, he he he?♪

Amy appears quite happy that you want to meet with her matango friend again, and readily takes you there at record speed, having no problem navigating her way through the Caverns until you've reached the now familiar nook where the mushroom woman awaits you.

Upon the two of you stepping into her somewhat cramped mushroom-covered home, she promptly rouses from the spot where she has remained comfortably rooted in place, and greets you with a smile, taking you in with visible fondness.
Amy and [ThePlayerName]! So good to see you two again, he he he... What brings you here today?♪
While she awaits your response, Amy casually trots by her side and restfully sits down, letting out a purr-like sound as your purple-capped hostess idly pats her head in reaction to her approach.
{i}Do you feel like there's something wrong?{/i} (Willpower)
Ask to see the matango's wares.
'How do you and Amy know each other?'
'What do you and Amy do together?'
'What's the difference between a matango and a toxic matango?'

EventText.NameOfScene: {i}Do you feel like there's something wrong?{/i} (Willpower)
theScene: Having experienced your fair share of deception, surprise attacks, and assorted trickery since you've set foot on Lucidia, you can't help but feel a {i}little bit{/i} wary at having been received so cheerfully and openly by the toxic matango, and thus deem it healthy to dedicate a moment to taking stock of your present circumstances, if only for the sake of your peace of mind.

For starters, you are reasonably sure that you, Amy, and your hostess are the only people present. The nook you're currently standing in is small enough as to provide no credible hiding spots, and it clearly has only one entrance, which would be hard to approach stealthily, making an ambush an unlikely possibility.

As for aphrodisiacs like matangos are wont to use... there {i}is{/i} a faint, pleasant scent hanging in the air, which seems to be exuded both by the purple-capped woman and by the many mushrooms layering the walls of her habitat, but it would be a stretch to call it any kind of 'poison'. For that matter, you don't feel especially aroused, flushed, or otherwise intoxicated, and are quite certain you're in full control of yourself.

All in all, even looking at your present situation under the most critical eye possible, you can't find anything that would be a cause for alarm... It really does seem like the monster girl is just minding her own business in her neck of the Caverns, and is genuinely happy to have visitors.
... And on that note, you should probably wrap up your inspection and move on with the conversation before your introspective silence starts actually getting awkward.

Considering that you've been invited into someone's home, your present circumstances feel like the best possible occasion to make full use of your charisma in order to be polite, courteous, likeable... In a word, the very model of a guest!

Accordingly, you get into the thick of chatting, and though you begin with innocuous small talk, you quickly move on to taking demonstrative interest in your hostess, her passions, and her hobbies, then deftly carry on by weaving in compliments about her, her home's arrangement, and the high opinion Amy has of her, all the while taking care not to come off as just obsequious.

And as a result, though the mushroom woman answers your praise with seeming nonchalance, you can see that she seems quite pleased with the attention, a very matango-like blush showing on her cheeks as she playfully bats her hand.
Oh my, he he he... Who would have thought Amy would bring me such a {i}charmer{/i}? Dear human, you really bring a touch of gentility to my humble abode, he he he.♪

While the aforementioned pale huntress purrs happily, seeming quite pleased that the two of you are getting along so well, the toxic matango considers you with a pensive expression, letting out a thoughtful hum that segues into her trademark giggle as she carries on.
He he he, let me tell you what: I wouldn't usually show something like this to anyone, but for a friend of Amy and such a {i}gentleman{/i} to boot, I'm willing to make an exception.♪

Taking on a conspiratorial air, she turns to the clusters of dark mushrooms covering one of her walls, and at an unseen signal from her, the fungi shift, part, and shrink, revealing a hitherto hidden shelf of sorts, the bare slab directly carved into the stone and holding a number of items, some of which look familiar to you while others seem more unique.
Tada, he he he! Feel free to have a look! Should anything interest you, let me know, and I might be persuaded to part with it in exchange for a few Eros, he he he.♪

While your hostess proudly crosses her arms and leans aside to let you inspect her wares, Amy takes a closer look with a curious coo, though she doesn't seem to be interested in doing more than window shopping, leaving you free rein to peruse the merchandise on display and see if anything might fit your adventuring needs.
Inspect the little mushroom-shaped lamp. (1500 Eros)

A small, but dangerous purple-capped mushroom from a toxic matango that can induce a variety of effects when ingested, almost all unpleasant. Can be used to make poisons, aphrodisiacs, and the occasional food dish. Can't induce monstrification in women.

EventText.NameOfScene: Inspect the little mushroom-shaped lamp. (1500 Eros)
theScene: A small, ornament-sized lantern in the shape of a mushroom. Attuning to this item reduces aphrodisiac damage you take by 10%, lowers Energy lost from drowsiness by 20%, and grants a 20% resistance to paralysis' energy cost amplification, as well as a 15% resistance against debuffs.
A rune made from fairy dust that helps ward off paralysis. Grants the wearer a stacking 10% paralysis resistance and a stacking flat 10% resistance to paralysis' energy cost amplification.

A rune infused with toxic matango magic, increasing the potency of its wearer's aphrodisiacs by 10%... while making them 10% more susceptible to aphrodisiacs! But it's fine, isn't it? Don't you just {i}love{/i} swimming in sweet-smelling bliss, hi hi hi?♥

An exceptionally scrumptious-looking mushroom from a toxic matango, sporting alluring cream-white and lavender-purple colors. Unlike a regular specimen, it looks edible and even quite tasty, suffused with the allure of its monster girl of origin... as well as her aphrodisiacs.

As you're about to give the matango the Eros for your chosen item, you realize that you actually don't have the money to afford it... Looks like you'll have to come back later, once you're more in funds.
The toxic matango giggles at your question, waggling her eyebrows knowingly as she answers.
He he he... A lady has her secrets, but suffice to say that even here, in the depths of Lucidia, there exist mercantile avenues through which a woman of means may procure articles of her choosing.♪

... And if nothing else, sufficiently adventurous, greedy, or kinky salarisi can be persuaded to venture to your Town or even as far as the Capital in exchange for a cut of the money or payment in {i}kind{/i}, he he he.♥
She again wiggles her eyebrows, this time more suggestively, hinting that there definitely are salarisi out there with a pronounced penchant for matango aphrodisiacs...
The toxic matango nods and lets the concealing curtain of mushrooms return to its initial position, giving you a content smile.

I hope you enjoyed this bit of impromptu shopping, he he he.♪ Should you be interested in my articles again, do not hesitate to ask me. I will be glad to support the endeavors of a good friend of Amy.

EventText.NameOfScene: Ask to see the matango's wares.
theScene: Looking as happy as ever to show off her selection of wares, the toxic matango silently commands some of the mushrooms covering the walls of her home to part, proudly revealing her neatly arranged collection of items.

EventText.NameOfScene: 'How do you and Amy know each other?'
theScene: The mushroom woman giggles fondly at the question, casting an affectionate glance at the eldritch huntress who lets out an uncustomarily shy little coo in response, looking to be a bit embarrassed by the topic, but nevertheless not stopping her friend from answering.
He he he, a good question.♪ After all, I am a literally rooted-in-place homebody, and the Caverns are not known for their... {i}thriving social sphere{/i}, hi hi hi...

So as you may already have surmised, Amy was the one who found me, he he he. You wouldn't believe it looking at her now, all spry and bold as brass, but I remember that at the time, she was quite the scaredy cat, always lurking in the dark and jumping at shadows... It was quite the test of patience to coax her closer and get a conversation going with her, he he he.♪

While the topic appears to be a source of nostalgic amusement for your hostess, your pale traveling companion meanwhile doesn't seem too fond of the recollection, looking pleadingly at the matango who takes mercy on her embarrassed torment and placates her with a doting side hug while going on in a less purposefully teasing tone of voice.

Anyhow... Long story short, once we got on speaking terms, we really hit it off, he he he.♪ We spent many an hour chatting about this and that, and if this humble agaric may give herself a pat on the back, I daresay that I contributed in no small measure to helping our lovely darling come out of her shell, hi hi hi.

Amy trills approvingly at her words, rubbing against her mushroom pal who returns the gesture with affectionate pats, but otherwise leaves her recounting at that, seeming to have filled you in on the long and short of their past.

However, as you think about what she just said and some of the terms she used, you can't help but get a nagging feeling of incongruity... 'Conversation', 'speaking', 'chatting'... Isn't Amy shy about talking, though, to the point of refusing to do so altogether unless strictly necessary?
Hmm...? Oh, yes, I see what you mean! He he he...

While she giggles proudly like a prestidigitator about to reveal the secret of a magic trick, Amy looks away shyly at the mention of her vocal difficulties, her purple-capped friend reacting to her embarrassment by giving her a gentle shoulder squeeze for reassurance as she goes on.

Well... In truth, 'talking' isn't exactly it, he he he... To explain, as you may or may not know, matangos, just like mushrooms, communicate by releasing signals and chemicals, which we can diffuse and understand more quickly and readily than words, just like so...

Sticking out her tongue in mock effort for emphasis, she does something you can't perceive, but which the mushrooms layering the walls around her react to, shaking, shivering, and bouncing in dance-like motions while Amy purrs happily as if she were listening to catchy music, wagging her tail enthusiastically as your hostess turns back to you.
... And as you must have figured now, he he he, our darling is just as receptive to those signals, which prove to be quite the surprise, but also quite the convenience for exchanging, hi hi hi.♪
Of course, he he he, this begs the question of how and why she is possessed of such an ability, but friends don't pry into each other's private business, hm?

Suddenly finding herself back in awkward territory, Amy blushes timidly at the mention, the matango not appearing to notice, but letting out a small exclamation as something seems to come back to her, though you suspect she is also doing so to steer the conversation away from the touchy topic.

Oh, and back to the subject of matango communication, we're obviously able to speak as well, and that's what we do when we communicate with humans or with most any other monster girl, but when we're between ourselves, we tend to prefer talking 'quietly' instead, he he he.

But others tend to find the sight of a bunch of matangos just standing in place and staring at each other in silence to be disturbing, sooo... we refrain from doing so when we have company, he he he...

Her chuckle comes off as a bit strained as she nervously fiddles with her natural cuffs, showing a rare moment of embarrassment which Amy reacts to by comfortingly chirruping and nuzzling her, the gesture seeming to be appreciated by the mushroom woman who gives her a gentle caress in return then turns back to you, that last mention seeming to have wrapped up the topic.

EventText.NameOfScene: 'What do you and Amy do together?'
theScene: Oh, mostly talk, he he he. As you'll get no points for having guessed, I don't move around much, so our little darling brings me news of the Caverns... Though in truth, we always end up talking about everything and nothing. You wouldn't imagine how {i}insatiable{/i} she can be, especially back when we were fresh acquaintances. She wouldn't stop asking questions! Quite the star pupil, that one, hi hi hi.♪
She smiles brightly to the eldritch monster girl who happily wags her tail at the praise while the toxic matango adds.

Oh! And of course, we play games, he he he... Board games, mostly, as you again won't be surprised to learn. Pebbles, especially... In fact, would you like to give it a try against Amy? I'm warning you, though: she's quite the {i}devil{/i} at it. I myself barely pose a challenge to her anymore, hi hi hi.

Your traveling companion almost tangibly radiates enthusiasm at the possibility, nearly hopping in place while her tail's shaking redoubles, the large limb flailing dangerously close to the innocent mushrooms that cover her friend's home's walls.

Trilling with joy, Amy fetches a purse filled with many little stones of different colors which she speedily distributes between the two of you, her caudal appendage's motions thankfully abating to a safer tempo of thrilled vibrations as she patiently waits for the matango to explain the rules to you.

Through the quick rundown you're given, you learn that the game is similar to chess in that it is played on a grid, with each color of pebble having a different range of motion, and the players' goal being to capture as many of their opponent's pieces as possible within a set number of turns.

Once you've been filled in, the two of you sit down and begin your match under the arbitration of your hostess who watches with interest while your opponent immediately locks in, her attention suddenly fully focused on the board with razor-sharp focus, hinting that this won't be an easy game...

And indeed it is not, Amy not only keeping shrewdly track of the placement of all the pebbles in play, but also catching you by surprise with all manners of stratagems, surprise maneuvers, and feints that quickly bring the demise of several of your valiant stones, the pale huntress swiftly carving out an advantage that will be hard to catch up with...

And unfortunately, it turns out that you don't have the chops to make a comeback, your competitor proving to be as mercilessly skilled in board gaming as she is in chasing, making short work of your remaining pieces and annihilating them all before you've even reached the turn limit, your fateful end getting met with a wince from the matango.
Ouch, that's a thorough thrashing, he he he...
But don't you feel bad: as I said, Amy is {i}really{/i} good at this, hi hi hi... But you can always give it another try another time?

Amy, for one, certainly looks enthusiastic at the prospect, being all smiles as she puts the pebbles back in their pouch in a swift and orderly manner. At least, she doesn't seem to be a sore winner, appearing to have simply enjoyed the fun of the game, and refraining from lording her victory over you, which at least makes your defeat {i}a bit{/i} less vexing than it might have been...

However, you don't let yourself get overwhelmed, and take your time to maintain a good overview of the game, plan your strategies, and anticipate your adversary's tricks... and little by little, you manage to turn back the tide, to the surprise of both Amy and the matango!

Firing on all cylinders, you carry on with your counter-offense, and though the pale monster girl doesn't take it lying down and strikes back with clever tactics of her own, you call upon all your smarts and creativity to retain the upper hand, the heated match proceeding in tense silence until, abruptly, after one more move on your part, your arbiter declares.
Aaaand {i}stop{/i}! End of the game... And that makes nine remaining pebbles against seven in our guest's favor, meaning that the human competitor is the victor, he he he!

Drawing back with a big sigh while the beaten huntress sadly coos at this conclusion, you run your hands over your head in relief, suddenly becoming aware of how concentrated you were and how demanding the game of wits was, especially compared to the kind of 'competition' Lucidia has made you used to. Your hostess definitely wasn't kidding when she praised her protege's aptitude...

But on that note, any sadness that Amy may have felt over her defeat quickly subsides as she happily extends her open palm to offer a handshake in quite the sportsmanlike fashion, seeming to be content to have met her match, and humming musically as she diligently puts the pebbles back into their pouch, wrapping up your intense session with no hard feelings.

Amy completely deflates at your refusal, her tail falling back limply while she looks away with an expression that plainly says 'Spoilsport.', the toxic matango meanwhile giving a fond giggle at her reaction, but not commenting on it, letting you decide what you'd want to do instead.
Hmm? Why, because I'm of a naturally cheerful disposition, of course, he he he.♪

Her smug-sounding titter rings eerily in her small nook, prompting a quizzical look from you as you wonder if she is just teasing you, your demonstration of puzzlement making her hum pensively as she considers your question more seriously.

Hmm... I see that you are skeptical, dear guest, but that {i}is{/i} the truth, he he he... Matangos such as I are fun-loving, and we genuinely enjoy having a good time, whether with fellow matangos, other monster girls, or humans, hi hi hi.♥

She coquettishly winks at you with an alluring wiggle of her hips at the last mention, {i}that{/i} kind of behavior seeming more in line with what you'd expect from a monster girl, though she appears to realize so herself, her expression switching back to a more sober one as she goes on.

Of course, humans might be wary of what we consider a 'good time', but we {i}do{/i} genuinely want nothing more than to give and receive pleasure, something which most humans come around to eventually.♥ And there is nothing better than a good fuck in the throes of an aphrodisiac-induced trance, he he he... So if you meet a matango, be a dear and go along with her fun, will you?♥

She waggles her eyebrows suggestively while letting out a small puff of purple-colored smoke that floats vaporously towards you, sending a faint but enticing pulse of heat through your body as you inhale the sweet scent, all the while Amy chirps approvingly at her friend's suggestion, though you have a {i}hunch{/i} that this isn't the kind of behavior that would be recommended or approved of on the part of a professional adventurer...

EventText.NameOfScene: 'What's the difference between a matango and a toxic matango?'
theScene: The mushroom woman scoffs at the question, displaying something approaching anger for probably the first time since you've met her.

Well, {i}first off{/i}, I somewhat take offense at the term 'toxic', hm hm hm... {i}Sure{/i}, our spores are more potent than those of 'regular' matangos, and {i}sure{/i}, they can cause female humans to turn into one of our own in case of repeated exposure... but that's hardly our fault, is it? {i}Hm hm hm{/i}, I swear, all it takes is for some overly imaginative scientist to coin the term, and all of a sudden, you're worse than the Demon Queen...

Letting out a rueful sigh while Amy placatingly pats her, she emphatically rolls her eyes at the whole idea, before returning her attention to you with a more composed expression, seeming to have gotten her frustrations off her chest.

But to answer your question... He he he, well, as I mentioned, our aphrodisiac is stronger than our more demure peers', and we are more aggressive as well, being less shy about showing our affection.♥

Yet, our romantic habits aside, we're really not that different, he he he. We both love dark, damp spaces, peace and quiet, and long, sustained, close-contact lovey-dovey mating.♥ Don't let the shyness of red matangos fool you, he he he, they're every bit just as interested in sex.♥

She hums lasciviously as she pronounces the last word, licking her lips with perverted relish at the brazen suggestion, though her lewd cooing transitions into a more pensive sound as she thoughtfully considers you.

For that matter, he he he, you may have noticed that us purple matangos are much better at pacing ourselves sexually... But I'll let you in on a little secret you might not know about: if you want to make one of us go {i}wild{/i}, go and have sex with a red matango. A {i}lot{/i} of sex. You may find yourself getting a {i}bit{/i} more into it than you expected, and might come out of it a {i}bit{/i} changed, but you won't regret it, hi hi hi.♥
She lets the sultry proposition hang in the air, but leaves it at that, letting you consider it on your own and, for the time being, move on to whatever else you want to talk about.
Of course.♪ I imagine you must have many more things to attend to. And far be it from me to buttonhole you, he he he...
Oh! But before you leave, hi hi hi, could I inquire as to your name, dear human and friend of Amy?♥

With nothing untoward having happened to you through your stay, and with your quadruped friend encouraging you with an enthusiastic expression and an enjoining chirrup, you figure that it can't do any harm, and so share your name with the affable toxic matango who nods.
[ThePlayerName] it is... Then have a good day, Amy and [ThePlayerName], and do feel free to swing by again whenever you feel like it, he he he.♪

The mushroom monster girl sees you off with a daintily cheerful wave that Amy joyfully responds to, your eldritch companion looking tickled pink that the two of you went to see her friend, prancing happily forward as you exit the toxic matango's lair and go back to your walk...

EventText.NameOfScene: You venture into a dark, echoing cave.
theScene: With the untalkative monster girl not providing much in the way of conversation, and with her definitely not needing you to look out for her, your attention ends up being mostly focused on your environment, letting you take in the idiosyncrasies of the subterranean biome that you've now gotten quite accustomed to, the Caverns holding less and less surprises for you by now.

In this fashion, you notice that wherever your wanderings are taking you, it seems to be a dark place, the ambient light gradually decreasing as the shining crystals around you grow sparser and sparser...

... until you are all but fumbling through the dark, the barest hint of remaining luminosity letting you see that you've entered what seems to be a much larger cave compared to the tunnel you were just walking through.

Unlike you, however, Amy is plainly not hindered by the obscurity and continues to progress without slowing her pace, her steady advance forcing you to somewhat bumblingly follow after her lest you get left behind. With her sleek pale form being barely visible in the gloom, you have to squint to even keep sight of her, making you seriously consider the option of just recommending that the two of you double back and take another path...
Yet, before you can do so, you notice her suddenly stopping, her quadruped body stealthily lying low against the ground as she gazes at something ahead with a predator's steady gaze!

Halting as well, you gauchely try to be as silent and motionless as possible in an effort to be just as inconspicuous, though you can't help but wonder whether your attempt is futile and you have already been spotted anyway as you peer at the blackness ahead, trying to see what Amy has seen...

And you {i}do{/i} make out something amid the darkness: a vaguely humanoid, thin, and probably feminine silhouette slowly wandering through the stalagmites that litter the area, the creature indistinctly muttering to itself as it zigzags back and forth across the cave, looking like it is talking to itself as it aimlessly meanders.

With your initial surprise, it takes you a moment to identify the shape, but after just a few seconds of getting your thoughts back in order, you quickly recognize it as Tabitha, the innocent bat girl clearly still being as shy and awkward as ever...

It thus seems like you aren't in any immediate danger, but the ball is nonetheless in your court, your close-by eldritch companion staying put as she casts you a questing side glance, as if to ask: 'What do we do now?'

... And what do you do indeed?

You signal to your porcelain friend to make a discreet getaway, and she silently nods in response, keeping her stare vigilantly trained on the wandering and whispering silhouette as she cautiously inches back the way you came from...

Carefully, you do the same, doing your best to avoid any obstacles you might trip or bump into as you retreat back to less darkened pastures, getting further and further away from the lone shape who still doesn't seem to have noticed either of you as you get away from it step by step...
... until you lose sight of it entirely and both you and Amy can relax as you leave the large cavernous place...
... and return to brighter parts, leaving the unexpected foray into the gloomy cave behind you.

With the tense moment having passed, the two of you can take some time to breathe out and chill for a bit... Then with not much else to do than exchange a look of understanding, you turn back and retrace your steps together, walking away from the forebodingly yawning chamber and taking another path to continue your exploration...

Figuring that you might as well make use of this opportunity to get the drop on the seemingly unsuspecting figure, you creep forward as silently as you can, though you quickly find out that your attempt is made quite complicated by the pebbles, stalagmites, and assorted debris that litter the wild place...

Silent as a stalking salaris, you patiently make your way onward, agilely slaloming between the obstacles on your way, your advance proceeding without incident under the eyes of the still alertly crouched Amy, until you have gotten very close to the shadowed figure... Close enough, in fact, that you would only need to reach out with your arm to touch it, and also close enough that you can make sense of its mutterings.

{size=-10}I-It's okay, Tabitha, keep it together... I-If someone comes by, y-you're not going to panic. Y-You're going to p-politely i-introduce yourself, g-get a conversation going, and m-make f-f-friends...!{/size}
Step away and approach her as if you'd just arrived.
Feeling mischievous and daring, you get right behind the unsuspecting Tabitha... and suddenly grab her shoulders with a raucous cry for added effect!
... But instead of turning around, flailing, or just shouting in surprise, the skittish woman jumps out of her skin, letting out an ear-splitting {b}shriek{/b}!

Louder than it has any right to be for someone her size, the deafening scream blasts outwards and reverberates cacophonously around the echoing chamber, leaving your vision suddenly blurring as something in your brain seems to seize, your limbs locking against your will!

Your entire body freezing, there is nothing you can do but watch as the apparently terrified silhouette hastily dashes off without even looking back, continuing to scream at the top of her lungs with every step...

Meanwhile, with your muscles stubbornly refusing to obey you, you can only ingloriously lose your balance and tumble over, fortunately on your side, resulting in you landing against your shoulder and getting away with no more than a bruise as you watch your hapless target sprint off in haste...
You {i}may{/i} have forgotten that Tabitha tends to do that when she is frightened... and that she gets frightened {i}very{/i} easily...

Being left paralyzed as you are, you can only wonder how long you're going to remain like this, the idea of staying immobile in the dark and at the mercy of any passing-by monster girl not being a very cheerful one...

However, to your relief, after a few minutes, you twitchingly feel that you can control your muscles again, and manage to laboriously sit up to rub your painful shoulder, a dolorous whine behind you informing you that Amy went through quite the same fate as you did, and that she did not much care for the experience, her face furrowed in discomfort as she stiffly stretches some life back into her limbs.

In the end, it only takes a moment more for the two of you to be back in active shape, which leaves you alone in the dark and now undoubtedly empty cave, your companion concluding your misadventure with a low coo that seems to say: 'Let's not do that again.'
Regardless, with the two of you now being on your own, the only thing left to do is leave this forebodingly somber place, returning to the safer-feeling brighter areas of the Caverns...
... where you can return to your peregrination...

EventText.NameOfScene: Step away and approach her as if you'd just arrived.
theScene: Figuring that the most no-nonsense option might be the better one, you silently double back to put yourself back within reasonable distance, then reveal your presence by stepping forward and calmly hailing the shadowed person, your eldritch companion going along with your choice by leaving her crouched posture and walking up to the stranger as non-threateningly as possible.

You do your best to remain undetected, but with the path ahead being barely visible to your eyes, you eventually stumble, hitting your shin squarely against a treacherously low stalagmite... And even though you manage to stifle a gasp as sharp pain shoots up your body, the dull sound of your untimely collision is enough to alert the lone figure who starts and looks around skittishly, its undoubtedly feminine voice rising shakily.
Going for broke, you abandon all subtlety and, powering through your banged leg, you pounce at your target!

Behind you, you sense that Amy is joining the charge, springing into action and launching herself forward at speed... But to your shared surprise, when the silhouette sees the two of you lunging for her, she seems to completely panic and lets out an ear-splitting {b}shriek{/b}!

Figuring that the most no-nonsense option might be the better one, you reveal your presence by stepping forward and calmly hailing the shadowed person, your eldritch companion going along with your choice by leaving her crouched posture and walking up to the stranger as non-threateningly as possible.

Yet, despite your peaceful approach, the lone figure appears wary and cringes away from you, seemingly spooked by Amy... or perhaps by you... or perhaps by the two of you? Whatever the reason for her skittishness may be, she swiftly and fearfully backpedals, stammering.
A-Ah, h-hello... Y-You- I-I mean I- Y-You- I-I'm not- I mean I need t-to... I... {i}SorryIhavetogo!{/i}

Before either of you and Amy can do or say anything, the darkness-veiled stranger spreads a pair of wings and hastily flaps away before your eyes, your pale companion looking quite crestfallen at your failed attempt at socialization and letting out a long, sad-sounding chirrup as she watches the unknown woman speed away in the air...

Looks like this wasn't the best of circumstances for you to meet... But maybe if you managed to encounter this winged solitary person in a different situation, you might be able to approach her better?
Before your joint advance, the darkness-veiled silhouette ahead of you flinches, but then lets out a little gasp of surprise as her posture shifts in realization.
Sighing in relief at your familiar presence, the bat girl comes forward into view, taking you in with round eyes that don't hide her surprise at unexpectedly finding you here.

However, as Amy innocently lets out a happy trill while enthusiastically wagging her tail at meeting a potential new friend, the timid woman loses her composure anew and flinches in discomfort at the eldritch creature's sight, her winged arms reflexively folding over her while her respiration nervously accelerates.

Sensing her agitation, your quadruped companion calms down and quickly makes herself smaller with an apologetic chirrup while Tabitha looks away and works on calming herself down, taking deep breaths while awkwardly fidgeting in place, you and your exploration partner patiently waiting for her to collect herself until the shy Cavern-dweller eventually volunteers.
O-Okay, it's okay now, I'm good...
Yet, as soon as the pale huntress makes another tentative attempt at approaching her, she immediately cringes away, her fragile assurance instantly crumbling all over again.

Dejectedly, she lets out a huff of self-reproach while pitifully hugging herself with her wings and staring at the ground in embarrassed silence for a while, though she eventually manages to gather enough gumption to look Amy's way and stammer.

I-I'm sorry, i-it's nothing against you, I swear... I-If you're a friend of [ThePlayerName], I-I'm sure you're very nice, b-but I'm, uhm... {i}really{/i} bad at dealing with new people... O-Or multiple people...

The porcelain woman looks quite bummed out at the verdict, but appears to be understanding, letting out a low coo of acknowledgment while drawing back and settling on her haunches with her tail wrapped around her in a posture that she endeavors to keep as non-threatening as possible, the display at least having the effect of keeping Tabitha's hair-trigger anxiety at bay.

Still, the resulting impasse makes for quite the awkward situation, the shy girl not even daring to look at either of you as she continues to squirm in place, her mind no doubt racing with nervous shame and jumbled thoughts, the uncomfortable stand-off ultimately prompting her to squeakily sigh out.
I-I think I'll just go... S-Sorry [ThePlayerName], Amy, I promise I-I'll keep working on my i-issues!

Glumly, she turns away and flies off, not even seeming to realize that she called your companion by her name even though she's never heard it before, the aforementioned monster girl keeping a straight face until the bat girl is gone, but then staring at her quickly receding shape with longing eyes and a plaintive whine.

Well, that could have gone better... After having been with her for quite some time now, you know how much your eldritch buddy likes making friends, and how poorly she handles rejection... If you want these two to get along better, maybe you could visit Tabitha one of these days and help her build up her confidence?

Yet, after that initial, skittish reaction, she appears to get a grip on herself and make an effort to rein in her anxieties, taking in a deep breath while your eldritch companion eyes her in slight confusion, the bat girl eventually managing to offer a slightly shaky smile and volunteer.
H-Hi, my name's Tabitha... Y-You must be a friend of [ThePlayerName]? ... Amy, right?
Ah, er, I mean- [TPN]-[ThePlayerName] told me... I-I didn't spy on your or a-anything, I swear!
W-Wait, how do I know that!? I-I didn't spy on you, I swear!

While Amy gives a good-humored warbling chuckle at her confusion, you decide that now is a good time to step in and take care of the introductions before Tabitha gets even more confused and potentially loses her nerve.

You begin by explaining to each of them who the other is and how you met her, both monster girls intently listening to you as you recount the circumstances of your initial meeting, looking to be quite interested in the subject and quickly getting hooked by your narration.

When you get to the part where Amy ambushed you and Perpetua, and go over the hapless slime girl's comical reaction, Tabitha can't keep herself from laughing out loud while the culprit trills cheerfully, seeming quite proud of her stunt and making no effort to stop you from describing her mischief in vivid detail.

As for the bat girl... You gloss over the lewder parts of your interactions with her so as not to embarrass her, but even then, she seems to feel rather shy about being the object of your attention, looking away with a blush on her face while your quadruped companion conversely looks to be quite enchanted by the topic, quietly listening and looking to the demure winged girl with indulgent fondness.

With the ice having thus been broken, a conversation ensues with surprising smoothness, the gloom of the area seeming to dissipate as you companionably carry on by exchanging anecdotes, news, rumors, and thoughts, as though you had gathered at some trendy meeting spot and not just coincidentally met in some desolate chamber...

And though you initially act as a sort of interpreter between the two new acquaintances, your mediation quickly becomes less and less necessary as they warm up to each other, seeming to rapidly bond over their social hardships and over being relative newcomers to the Caverns, the initially awkward atmosphere rapidly melting away and making way for warm fellowship.

In fact, before you know it, the three of you are sitting on the floor and casually chatting, the two monster girls getting along so well that Tabitha eventually proves so bold as to curiously touch Amy's face, her long fingers experimentally gliding over the mask-like surface as she wonders aloud.
Wow, it's really amazing how seamless your mouth is... And how smooth your face is, too!
A-Ah, sorry! I shouldn't have t-touched you without asking! S-Sorry!

But with a happy chirp, the eldritch huntress lets her know that she doesn't mind, and that her new friend is in fact quite welcome to continue, her tendriled hair grabbing at her black hand and gently pulling it back towards her, the gesture slightly startling the timid woman who exhales in nervous shyness but nonetheless goes along with the suggestion.

Thus does the bat girl oblingingly continue to playfully caress, pat, and tap at Amy's face with her dexterous fingers while the pale predator leisurely lounges in her lap, trilling so happily and continuously as to sound like she's purring, the relaxing sound further contributing to the genial atmosphere that has settled over your little group.

Little by little, the clock ticks as your unlikely trio continues to swap pleasantries, minutes turning into hours as you lose track of time, something which proves quite easy when you're in such good company and in a place where the sun doesn't shine, day and night always looking the same in the Caverns...

Until suddenly, after you finish an engrossing retelling of your encounter with the salaris posse, Tabitha gasps in realization, the pause in the conversation seeming to make her notice you've been at it for quite a while now.
O-Oh, gosh, how long has it been!? I-I left a painting to dry at home! I have to go back now and apply the next layer or it won't look right!-

In a fluster, she gets up, letting go of Amy who flexibly rolls away from her lap pillow while profuse apologies spill out of the sputtering artist for bailing on you, though you and your quadruped companion quickly cool her down by assuring her that it's okay and that you can always meet again, your placating response promptly defusing her panicked agitation.
Thus reassured, Tabitha quiets down, and trades her squeaking anxiety for a relieved smile, taking in the two of you with genuine happiness plain on her face.
W-Well... Thank you for coming and for bearing with me, [TPN]-[ThePlayerName], Amy. I-I had a really good time!

With heartfelt words and chirrups, you reply that the same goes for you, and bid her goodbye as she draws back to get some space, then offers you a friendly wave before flying away, quickly disappearing into the darkness.

This leaves you once more alone with Amy who seems positively enchanted with how this encounter went, turning to you with overjoyed wags of her tail and giddy warbling as she dances around you in celebration for the fortuitous meeting, as well as in thanks to you for acting as a mediator.

Looks like you contributed to sparking a new friendship!

As minutes pass and you keep advancing from tunnel to chamber, you realize that Amy is aiming for a specific destination, one that you are now quite acquainted with: the dark echoing cavern where you initially met Tabitha, the location being easily recognizable by its formidable size and eerily gloomy atmosphere.

But your quadruped companion doesn't stop there, instead enthusiastically and unhesitatingly crossing the dreary-looking cave to get to its far side, and from there, unfailingly home in on the familiar set of stairs snaking across the uneven rocky wall, all the way up to the bat girl's home.

Having already had trouble following after the night-visioned eldritch predator through the obstacle-strewn blackness, you can't say that you're ecstatic at the idea of having to climb all the way up to your winged friend's house, {i}and in the dark at that{/i}, but with the cheerful wagging of Amy's tail urging you on, you resolve yourself to the trek and begin your ascent.

As expected, it's quite the workout... And also as expected, the indefatigable huntress doesn't even seem winded by the end of it, continuing to joyfully beckon you further as she nods towards the entrance to Tabitha's house, which she knocks at without a moment's delay while you regain your breathing.
There's a brief pause, followed by shuffling sounds, then the door opens, revealing a familiar shadowed shape which stutters.
O-Oh, it's you! ... A-And [ThePlayerName]? L-Let me get the light!

The bat girl quickly patters back inside, then a bright glow appears from within her home, finally letting you see clearly while Amy lets out a somewhat uncomfortable high-pitched squeak at the sudden light while Tabitha returns, taking you both in with a shy smile.
There... S-So... What brings you to my, er, h-humble abode?

Recovering from the unpleasant luminous glare with a head shake, your nonchalant guide comes forward, letting out a cheery chirrup that seems to say 'I just felt like it.', and which the demure monster girl reacts to with a small giggle.
Just a friendly visit, then? W-Well, feel free to let yourselves in. We can spend a moment together and... ch-chat?

Your porcelain companion doesn't need to be told twice and breezily lets herself in, followed by you, the two of you thus finding yourselves as guests of your winged hostess who closes the door behind you then nervously goes on to make sure you're comfortable in her ever-slightly-messy but nonetheless quite homey place.

Though she initially proves awkward, stuttering and fidgeting quite a lot as she doubtless buckles under the fear of being boring or unpleasant or weird, the bat lady's discomfort quickly melts away once a conversation gets going, and in short notice, the three of you are settled on her couch and enjoying a hearty snack of mangoes, Amy looking to be very much enjoying the exotic fruit as she digs into its succulent flesh with relish.

Juicy eating aside, you spend your stay at Tabitha's house just chewing the fat as the discussion bounces back and forth between you three, the usually nervous girl soon looking to have completely forgotten about her past reservations and getting quite into the exchange, talking with gusto and even getting swept into enthusiastic gabbing, going into long spirited speeches and laughing heartily, a far cry from her initial stuttering anxiety.

Nevertheless, it eventually appears that either the socialization is taking more out of her than expected, or she was more tired than she let on when she received you, the bat girl proving to be a less and less active participant in your talks, until she ends up closing her eyes and starts idly snoozing... only to rouse a moment after in a panicked fluster.
Buh-ah!? D-Did I fall asleep? S-Sorry, I stayed up a lot, a-and I-I was actually thinking of going to bed when you arrived, so-

While your hostess squeaks in embarrassment at her stammering confession, her pale visitor chuckles indulgently, both she and you assuring her that it's fine, and that you have no problem with leaving her be if she needs rest.

Though she still looks quite mortified at having allowed herself to doze off in front of her guests, Tabitha appears to appreciate your understanding and gets up from her seat to take you to her door, seeing you out with a fond smile.
Th-Thank you for coming, I had a really nice time... F-Feel free to come back whenever you want!

You tell her that you won't hesitate to, Amy nodding vigorously in approval to your words while animatedly wagging her tail, your demonstrations of friendly eagerness putting a timid blush right back on the shy girl's face...

... but clearly not seeming to be a source of displeasure for her, as you can rest assured when she closes the door only to let out a giddy muffled squee as soon as you're out of sight, your quadruped friend answering the 'hidden' demonstration of joy with a thrilled warble of her own while her caudal appendage's merry flailing redoubles, her overt gaiety forming quite the contrast with the gloomy outside of Tabitha's home.
It definitely feels like these two are getting along, and that the company is doing wonders for their mood and prospects. Who knows what the future has in store for them?

EventText.NameOfScene: You end up on a lumiren's hunting spot.
theScene: This time, your outing is taking you to an especially deep part of the underground, the stone around you gradually becoming blacker while your environment grows darker and damper, giving your surroundings a more ancient, downright primeval aspect that cannot but arouse your curiosity. It's like you're stepping into a place where no one has ever been before, giving you an oddly comforting sensation of uninhibited solitude...

It feels like, for once, your trip is going to be a wholly peaceful one and you can afford to loosen up a bit, the strangely serene atmosphere encouraging you to relax your guard and carry on your way with a more easygoing gait, enjoying the sights and sounds of the Caverns with your silent pale friend lightly padding alongside you.

The {i}sounds{/i} in particular feel especially pleasant, something about the acoustics of this place having a soothing effect on you, making you progressively zone out until you find yourself just woozily staring into space while your legs automatically carry on walking, as if you were caught in a cozy, fluffy daydream...

In fact, the more you keep advancing, the more at ease you feel, as if your head were filling more and more with soft cotton, making you feel {i}so{/i} comfortable and unbothered as you continue to progress towards-

Suddenly, you startle as you realize that something is tugging urgently at your leg, and blearily looking down, you can see Amy staring up at you from her crouched quadruped position, one of her clawed hands gingerly clutching your calf while her face scrunches up in concern as you confusedly take her in.

Blinking, you feel your thoughts laboriously return to clarity, your body staggering in place as your legs abruptly cease carrying you forward on their own, your befuddled mind dazedly trying to compute what just happened.

As if in answer to your unspoken question, your eldritch companion stares at the path ahead and lowly hisses in warning, her demonstration of caution spurring you to focus on your senses... and pick up on the sounds of sweet, delightfully enthralling singing you didn't realize you were hearing so far, the melliflous melody gently flowing around and within you, so pleasant to listen to...

Blinking once more, you catch yourself with your feet beginning to move anew without your conscious say-so, your involuntary advance getting again halted by the porcelain huntress who glances the way you came from with some urgency, seeming to consider it wiser to make yourselves scarce while you're still in possession of yourself.

Actually, even now, you feel the {i}need{/i} to keep going and get to the source of the wonderful melodious sounds floating towards you, and it's taking a conscious effort not to let yourself sink back into blissful stupor... but you at least can still exert enough control over yourself to make a decision.
Just keep going towards the singing.
Just keep going towards the singing.
'Couldn't you go ahead and... make it stop?'

Yielding to your worried friend's injunctions, you get a grip on yourself and nod, following after her as she hurries away, back to safer parts of the Caverns where you can take a quick break to refocus yourself, then resume your interrupted foray...

EventText.NameOfScene: Just keep going towards the singing.
theScene: As soon as you begin to move, Amy once more holds you back, again calling you to order with a wary chitter, nodding at the path ahead then back the way you came from with insistence... before thinking on it for a bit, then ultimately seeming to reach some sort of inner conclusion and letting go of you with a demure-sounding chirrup.
It's clear that if you were to go ahead and proceed regardless of her warnings, she would not stop you, but neither would she accompany you.
'Couldn't you go ahead and... make it stop?'

The pale monster girl looks perplexed as you give in for good and just let your legs resume carrying you away towards the origin of the enchanting singing, but makes no further efforts to stop you, just casting you one final glance before scuttling away, leaving you to your own devices.

Now on your own, you keep going, filled with a mixture of curiosity, arousal, and blissfulness, as well as some trepidation which nonetheless fades away as you yield to the magnetic voice and hurry on its way...
In answer to your question, your eldritch partner points to where her ears would be if she had any, then makes a waving up-and-down motion with her hand, then finally shrugs.
That... wasn't very illuminating, but at least, that gives you the confirmation that whatever the reason, the hypnotic singing has no effect on her.

EventText.NameOfScene: 'Couldn't you go ahead and... make it stop?'
theScene: You get a curious look in response to your query, as if you'd just asked something odd... or perhaps as if Amy hadn't thought about it?

Regardless, she doesn't seem actually adverse to the idea, shrugging away her apparent reservations before nodding in agreement, then pulling you away until you're out of hearing range of the mesmerizing sounds towards which she scurries off, though not before giving you a firm gaze then decisively pointing to the ground at your feet in a way that clearly means 'Stay here.'

Thus having been given your instructions, you wait alone for a minute... which turns into a few minutes... which themselves turn into {i}more{/i} minutes, the monotonous hold-up starting to make you feel frustrated... and even possibly a bit concerned...

Until your awaited companion returns, her gait nonchalant but her expression seemingly a bit tense as she signals for you to follow her with a low warble, to which you comply without delay, feeling all the more curious about what you'll be met with based on Amy's unexpected behavior.

After a short walk through an increasingly damp passage, you reach a rather familiar-looking chamber mostly occupied by a large natural basin in which floats an equally familiar-looking monster girl, the pouting woman treating you to a pointed glare as you walk into her quarters, before crossing her arms with an indignant huff.
{i}Hmph!{/i} There you are! Well... I behaved, so let's get this over with: ask your questions, and then you can leave me alone.

Though you unmistakably recognize the singing voice, you can't help but find the petulant griping to be a far cry from the previous dulcet tones, the lumiren's last words being apparently intended for you as much as for your porcelain friend who nonetheless pays them no mind, restfully sitting nearby and letting you handle the conversation... while keeping an alert eye on the pool-dweller.
'Why do you lure people with your singing?'
'What do you do with your days?'
'I didn't want to ask you anything, though?'
'Why do you lure people with your singing?'
'What do you do with your days?'

EventText.NameOfScene: 'I didn't want to ask you anything, though?'
theScene: Looking speechless at your innocent answer, the lumiren turns to Amy with an angry scowl.
And he didn't even actually want to talk about anything!? Thanks for holding me for {i}nothing{/i}!

With nary a goodbye, the aquatic monster girl turns away and dives down into the depths of the subterranean lake where her faintly glowing form quickly vanishes from sight amid the turbid waters, her moody departure making quite the splash that leaves you and Amy spattered with droplets of cold water.

Letting out a morose-sounding chirrup at that abrupt conclusion, Amy shakes herself dry, her evasive gaze making it rather plain that things didn't go the way she expected. You can't help but feel like some wires got crossed...
Nevertheless, there's not much to be done about it now, so after drying yourselves off as best you can, the only thing the two of you can do is move on and carry on with your exploration...

How could I {i}not{/i} be annoyed? There I was, just minding my own business, and out of nowhere comes Amy who interrupts my singing, won't leave me alone, and to top it all off, {i}insists{/i} on keeping me here because a human wants to ask me questions or something?!

While Amy's knowing glare makes it rather plain that she knows the aforementioned singing was not as {i}innocent{/i} as the aggravated songstress made it sound, the lumiren seems to mull over her own words, and appears to reach a conclusion she actually rather enjoys, her irked scowl unexpectedly turning into a sweetly placated smile as she happily adds.

Although... Thinking about it, I guess it's {i}flattering{/i} that a visitor is interested in {i}me{/i}... So I {i}guess{/i} you can go ahead and ask your questions, and I will do my best to sate your curiosity, human.♪
Your eldritch companion rolls her eyes at the sudden change of mood, the suddenly sunny-looking monster girl leaning forward affably and adding with a wink.

But still, for future reference, next time you come across a recital of mine {i}and there's no interlopers around to spoil the mood{/i}, do come and listen, dear human, I guarantee you an unforgettable performance.♪

The way that she alluringly singsongs the last words, as well as the way she lecherously licks her lips, leave you with little doubt as to the sexual nature of said 'performance', Amy reacting to the sultry proposition with a flat look, but offering no further unspoken comments on the subject, letting you carry on with your inquiries without objections.

Yes!... Well, not {i}here{/i} here, but I indeed reside in these Caverns. They're pleasantly quiet, the ambient light is easy on the eyes, the pressure and acoustics are quite agreeable, and they feature underground waterways aplenty, providing quite the convenient way of traveling around for someone like me.♪

She elegantly sways back and forth as she speaks, as if to make her meaning more explicit, the harmonious movements of her flexible frame showing off her tentacled lower body while her lilting speech gives way to a pensive hum as she seems to give further thought to the topic.

Actually, while we're on the subject, I did try giving the open waters a shot in the past, but... {i}Too much competition.{/i} You see, any human falling off a boat or venturing into the sea is fair game, so whenever there's prey available, it turns into quite the free-for-all.♪

Although her voice is as smoothly musical as before, her expression of overt relish belies her casual tone, the aquatic monster girl seeming to have quite {i}vivid{/i} memories of such occasions, though she rather quickly eases back into a more placid mien as she concludes.

... So in the end, I found that this place was more my speed. It hardly offers a cornucopia of adventurers, but that is fine: the ambience is rather enjoyable, and the occasional visitor is {i}quite the treat{/i}.♪
She finishes by staring at you with an air of undisguised hunger, clearly hinting that {i}you{/i} could be such a visitor...

EventText.NameOfScene: 'Why do you lure people with your singing?'
theScene: She brazenly answers with a sultry little trill in those words, the slightly tremulous purr of her voice seeming to enticingly flit about your hearing and coaxingly caress your mind, making your thoughts momentarily blank out as she goes on.

The precious thrill of a handsome human willingly bringing themself to you, getting enchanted by your voice and coming to you full of warm infatuation and desire, ready to leap into your arms and {i}mate{/i} indulgently with you for hours on end... No other sensual delight can compare.♪

The light in her eyes and the melodious tone of her voice grow downright captivating as she invitingly holds out one of her tentacles towards you, prompting you to unthinkingly take a step forward... then abruptly stop as you're jolted back into awareness by a piercing hiss from Amy that startles both you and your would-be charmer who gruffly pouts.
A-Alright, alright! I wasn't actually going to enthrall him, I was just... {i}demonstrating{/i}.

Your quadruped companion seems to doubt that as she skeptically sticks out her tongue, as do you as you woozily recover from the entrapping mellifluous fascination you'd been lured into, shaking your head as the lumiren innocently turns back to you.

{i}Ahem{/i}... But to conclude on the subject of my {i}beautiful singing{/i}... Well, aside from personal preference, there is also obviously the matter of practicability. As you've no doubt noticed, unlike you humans, I'm no land-dweller, and although I can bear with the open air for some time just fine, I'd be at quite the staggering disadvantage on dry ground... Hence this 'siren song' method.
Playfully, she lets one of her tentacles stick out of the water and wiggles it for emphasis, which seems to wrap up her explanation.

EventText.NameOfScene: 'What do you do with your days?'
theScene: Mmmh, aside from preying on cute humans like you, you mean?♪
She offers a lascivious giggle to punctuate the rhetorical question, but quickly smoothes back into a more composed expression as she carries on more casually.

Well... Aside from practicing my singing, I have something of a fondness for... treasure-hunting, I think you could call it? A lot of things wash out down here from many parts of Lucidia, including but not limited to the open sea, that one kunoichi hideout at the top of the Mountain, and obviously the Capital.

Of course, most of it is trash, but among all the dross, one can find the occasional piece of sea glass, the odd pretty bottle or can, or sometimes even a more outlandish trinket or component... Not that I'm much versed in technology myself, but I still keep some, out of a collector's impulse.♪
She singsongs cheerfully at that, seeming to have a genuine fondness for the hobby, though her smile grows more mischievous as she adds.

Oh, and back to my singing... Naturally, the appeal of my performances is not limited to humans. Other monster girls are just as susceptible to it, and can make for {i}very{/i} enjoyable partners. Salarisi in particular seem to be quite vulnerable to it, and make for {i}adorably vigorous{/i} lovers with flexibility and energy to spare...♥
She knowingly snickers at that, seeming to expect that you'll be sharing her taste, though she becomes thoughtful as her gaze errs Amy's way.
... Yet, {i}some{/i} salarisi seem to be outliers, looking to be much less... {i}amenable{/i}.
She curiously and suspiciously eyes the eldritch monster girl who just chuckles to herself in response, seeming to have no issue with being such a source of mystery and confusion.

Approaching the edge of the basin, you gesture for the lumiren to approach, the monster girl reacting to your unexpected request with a puzzled expression matched by Amy's questioning glance, but nevertheless going along with it, floatily getting within arm's reach of you.

Immediately seizing this opportunity, you raise your hand to pat her head, and have the happy surprise to find that despite her water-dwelling habits, the octopus-like woman's hair is not musty or clumped or slippery, instead feeling pleasantly smooth and fine against your fingers as you run your hand across it, affectionately mussing her long blue locks.

While she initially remains stunned before your audacious initiative, helplessly sputtering in flustered surprise while your eldritch companion playfully giggles behind you, she is quick to recover with an outraged pout, swiftly slapping your intrusive hand away with her finned one while angrily sputtering.
Wh-Who gave you the right!? Y-You- You {i}rascal{/i}!

Fuming, she immediately dives back into the water without a word more, making sure to copiously splash you as she takes her indignant leave, her furious exit leaving you sodden with cold cavern water... but filled with the satisfaction of having patted her! And judging by Amy's joyfully approving expression, it seems that your pale accomplice found that very funny, even though she got drenched just as much as you were.

But whether that was a good decision or not, there's nothing for it now but to dry yourselves off as much as you can, then carry on with your trek, since it doesn't look like the angry lumiren is going to come back anytime soon.
Thus departing from the premises with your clothes damp but your spirit undoused, you continue your trip...
Nodding, the lumiren stretches with a melodious hum, eyeing you and Amy thoughtfully.
Mmh... Well, this wasn't so {i}dreadful{/i} as I feared... I guess a bit of rubbing elbows isn't so bad, every now and then.♪
... But all the same, {i}do{/i} pay me a {i}personal{/i} visit one of these days, dear human. I'd love to have some {i}alone time{/i} with you.♥

Letting the alluring proposition hang teasingly in the air, she lets herself sink into the underground waters and quickly disappears without a trace, leaving you once more alone with Amy who likewise seems quite satisfied with how that encounter went, sporting an expression of quiet content as you move on from the now vacant subterranean lake and go back to your walk...

You try to apologize for whatever you did to make her angry, and for a moment you think your words fall on deaf ears, but she stops growling and looks off to the side with a loud 'Hmph!' before stalking towards you and sitting up on her hind legs.

Amy then points to her mouth as it lets out a resounding crack, splitting open horizontally across her mask like-face, her jaw practically unhinging as it opens and splits open vertically like porcelain mandibles, then her five {i}long{/i} squirming light purple tongues slither out of her mouth, drooling a toxic looking violet saliva.
